name: Tokenization Playground
summary: See how raw text gets split into tokens — the atoms of every LLM.
description: Type or paste any text and watch a simple whitespace-and-punctuation tokenizer split it into tokens. Token count updates live so you can build intuition for how context windows and API costs are measured.

name: Embedding Visualizer
summary: Compare two pieces of text with a mock cosine-similarity score.
description: Embeddings turn text into high-dimensional vectors. This playground computes a deterministic mock embedding for two short strings and reports their cosine similarity so you can see how semantically related the texts appear to a model.

name: Prompt Engineering Playground
summary: Compose a system prompt + user prompt and preview a mock response at different temperatures.
description: Temperature controls how random or deterministic a model's outputs are. Adjust the slider and observe how a mock generator varies its response shape — a great way to internalise the temperature knob before you spend API credits.

name: Context Window Simulator
summary: Visualize how prompts and responses consume a model's context budget.
description: Pick a context length and a per-request budget to see how many messages you can fit before the window fills up. Useful for understanding why long conversations get truncated.

name: Quantization Calculator
summary: Estimate the on-disk size of a model at different quantization levels.
description: Quantization trades precision for size. Enter a parameter count and slide the bits-per-weight to see the estimated file size in MB and GB.

name: RAG Simulator
summary: Drop in a tiny corpus and a query to see retrieval-augmented generation in action.
description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pairs a vector or keyword search over your own documents with a language model. Paste a short corpus and a question; the playground chunks the corpus, scores each chunk by keyword overlap, and stitches the top matches into a mock answer so you can see the retrieval step in isolation.

name: Vector Search
summary: Rank a document collection by mock-embedding similarity to a query.
description: Vector search powers semantic retrieval. This playground builds a mock embedding for each document by hashing its word tokens, then ranks them by cosine similarity to your query. Useful for feeling out how recall and ranking change with phrasing.

name: Agent Workflow Simulator
summary: Pick an order of agent steps and watch a mock execution unfold.
description: AI agents combine planning, search, reasoning, action, and answer steps. Toggle the steps you want, reorder them, then run a mock execution that reports status for each stage — a low-stakes way to design an agent loop before wiring up real tools.

name: Model Comparison Playground
summary: Pick two models from the atlas and compare their key attributes side by side.
description: Side-by-side comparison is the fastest way to choose between open models. Select any two entries from the models seed data and inspect parameters, modality, license, and tags in a single diff view.

name: Inference Visualizer
summary: Estimate tokens-per-second and VRAM use from prompt length and model size.
description: Local inference performance is dominated by model size and prompt length. Enter a prompt size and a parameter count to see a simple estimate of throughput and memory footprint — enough to compare 7B, 13B, and 70B runs without spinning up a GPU.

name: Fine-tuning Simulator
summary: Watch a mock loss curve respond to dataset size, epochs, and learning rate.
description: Fine-tuning is sensitive to dataset size, epoch count, and learning rate. Adjust the sliders and watch a deterministic mock loss curve evolve across training steps to build intuition for overfitting and convergence.

name: Diffusion Playground
summary: Trigger a mock diffusion run with prompt, steps, and CFG scale.
description: Diffusion models generate images by iteratively denoising random noise. Set a prompt, the number of denoising steps, and the classifier-free guidance scale to see a mock generation timeline and a placeholder caption describing the would-be output.

name: Whisper Transcription Demo
summary: Simulate an audio upload and inspect segmented transcript output.
description: Whisper transcribes speech into timestamped segments. Paste a transcript (or a JSON-shaped mock) to see it sliced into time-stamped segments with word counts, mimicking Whisper's verbose_json output.
